title: ZXPong Game Modes
description: Compare ZXPong Solo play and World PVP. World PVP is the multiplayer option currently available in the documented build.
summary: ZXPong live play is centered on Solo play and World PVP.

sections.heading: Solo play
paragraphs: Solo play is the practical starting point for learning paddle control, rally timing, and scoring rhythm.
Difficulty levels 1 through 10 shape how aggressively the AI responds.

sections.heading: World PVP
paragraphs: World PVP is the visible multiplayer route in the current live build.
Use World PVP after you are comfortable with paddle timing and can keep rallies stable under pressure.

sections.heading: Local PVP status
paragraphs: Local PVP is planned for a later update; World PVP is the multiplayer route currently available.
This documentation does not present Local PVP as a public mode until the release configuration changes.

faq.question: Is Local PVP available right now?
faq.answer: No. World PVP is currently available. Local PVP will be introduced in a later update.

featureShowcases.heading: ZXPong game modes
featureShowcases.summary: Game Modes explains how ZXPong structures paddle rallies, point scoring, local play, and supported competitive surfaces.
body: This matters because players need to know what kind of session they are starting and how success is measured.
Players use this page to choose the right mode for a quick game, a focused practice run, or a score-chasing session.
bullets: Learn how mode choice to controls, tips, and leaderboards.
Keeps App Store-friendly wording focused on gameplay.

image.caption: ZXPong mode artwork supports the game mode explanation.
